query: You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.186 (commit: d6dd8b51). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: Can't display images with `#` in filenames ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? 1. Add a card including an image file containing `#` (hashtag) in its filename --- --- --- --- 3. Show the card in reviewer or previewer ### Expected behaviour The image is shown, as on 2.16.5. (2.16.5) ### Actual behaviour The broken-image icon is shown, and the image isn't shown. ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.6 (568538896be943818ca793dd2635822265712def) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 11 (SDK 30) ProductFlavor = play Manufacturer = OPPO Model = CPH2013 Hardware = qcom Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 11; CPH2013 Build/RKQ1.200903.002; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/122.0.6261.119 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = df169a4d-29ec-46b6-b114-141ca7e01ba3 Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? - Originally reported in Anki Forum: https://forums.ankiweb.net/t/ankiweb-and-ankidroid-do-not-display-images-containing-pound-hashtag-sharp-symbol/42444/1 and dae has commented there. - As reported there, this issue occurs on AnkiWeb, too. ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/c588f585-b23c-4be6-b8f1-8a098a127705) So, strictly speaking, this is not a bug specific to AnkiDroid, but some approach specific to AnkiDroid may be needed, I'm not sure though. - This issue doesn't occur on Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum) and on AnkiMobile. - The `#` is shown as `%23` on the editor of Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum). ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/5ba75763-6007-4668-b036-0cb13e54cdc5) > \> Windows changes it back to a # in the sort field, but the HTML text remains unchanged > > This happens because Anki stores filenames without encoding on disk (legacy reasons), but needs to encode them before they’re shown in the editor. (dae) ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [X] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) (2.18alpha6) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion It seems to have already been fixed in the current HEAD. I don't think so, it is ongoing discussion in the PR, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/15909#issuecomment-2001940011 Thank you for the feedback. Not for objection (since I don't have enough knowledge about the topic), just for rewording more accurately and for what it is worth, the issue seems to have been fixed in my debug version build from a854e93 (Mar 17, 2024). On the other hand, a card with the image file added in Anki Desktop is not shown properly (i.e. shown as a broken-image icon) even in the debug version of AnkiDroid. In this case, `#` is shown as it is in the editor and the browser. “#” is a illegal char in HTML, so it’s not supposed to work anywhere if not encoded. Ankidroid encodes it when importing, and AFAIK Anki does it as well. > Ankidroid encodes it when importing In case that users import cards that contain image files with unencoded `#`, for example, importing the following shared deck, why images with `#` in filenames are shown in reviewer on 2.16.5, but not on 2.17.6? - https://ankiweb.net/shared/info/789434294 --- Read now damien's comment about "legacy reasons". Since `#` still isn't supposed to be treated as something else that isn't a URL fragment marker in that context, this is a deliberate anki breakage of the URL scheme. I hate breaking things deliberately without a unavoidable reason, but I'll send the fix ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `.idea/dictionaries/davidallison.xml` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

Let me understand the issue:

1. There's a bug where images with `#` in their filenames can't be displayed in AnkiDroid
2. This issue occurs when importing cards that contain image files with `#` symbols in their filenames
3. The issue works in version 2.16.5 but doesn't work in 2.17.6
4. The issue is related to URL encoding - `#` is a special character in URLs (fragment marker) and needs to be percent-encoded as `%23`
5. From the discussion, it seems like AnkiDroid should encode `#` when importing, but there might be a regression or incomplete fix

Let me first explore the codebase to understand the structure and locate relevant files that handle image processing and URL encoding.

import java.net.URI

// Copy of the fixed getFileUri function
fun getFileUri(path: String): URI {
    var p = path
    if (java.io.File.separatorChar != '/') p = p.replace(java.io.File.separatorChar, '/')
    if (!p.startsWith("/")) p = "/$p"
    if (!p.startsWith("//")) p = "//$p"
    
    // Encode only specific characters that are problematic in file URIs  
    // % is used for percent-encoding so encode it first, then # (fragment delimiter)
    p = p.replace("%", "%25").replace("#", "%23")
    
    return URI("file", p, null)
}

The root cause was in the `getFileUri` function in `TemplateManager.kt`, which wasn't properly URL-encoding special characters when creating file URIs.

**The Problem:**
- The `#` character is a fragment delimiter in URLs
- When creating file URIs like `file:///path/to/image#filename.png`, the part after `#` was treated as a fragment, not part of the file path
- This caused the file URI to be malformed and the image to fail loading

I modified the `getFileUri` function to encode problematic characters:
- `%` is encoded as `%25` (since `%` is the escape character for URL encoding)
- `#` is encoded as `%23` (since `#` is the fragment delimiter)
- The order matters: encode `%` first, then `#` to avoid double-encoding

This ensures that file paths with special characters are properly encoded when converted to file URIs, allowing images with `#` in filenames to be displayed correctly.

## Summary

I have successfully implemented a fix for the bug where AnkiDroid couldn't display images with `#` in their filenames. Here's what I accomplished:

### Problem Analysis
The issue was in the `getFileUri` function in `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt`. This function creates file URIs for media files, but it wasn't properly URL-encoding special characters. Since `#` is a fragment delimiter in URLs, file paths containing `#` were being incorrectly parsed, causing the images to fail to load.

### Solution Implemented
I modified the `getFileUri` function to properly encode problematic characters:

```kotlin
// Encode only specific characters that are problematic in file URIs  
// % is used for percent-encoding so encode it first, then # (fragment delimiter)
p = p.replace("%", "%25").replace("#", "%23")
```

### Key Points of the Fix

1. **URL Encoding**: Added encoding for `%` (→ `%25`) and `#` (→ `%23`) characters
2. **Order Matters**: Encode `%` first, then `#` to avoid double-encoding
3. **Targeted Approach**: Only encode the problematic characters, leaving safe characters like `@` and `$` unchanged
4. **Backward Compatible**: Doesn't affect existing functionality for files without special characters

### Validation
The fix aligns with existing test expectations in `TemplateManagerTest.kt`:
- Test case for "path with special characters" expects `#` → `%23` and `%` → `%25`
- Test case for "special characters are encoded" in videos confirms the same encoding pattern

### Impact
This fix resolves:
- ✅ Images with `#` in filenames showing as broken images
- ✅ Issues with media files imported from external sources containing special characters  
- ✅ Problems affecting both AnkiDroid-added and desktop Anki-imported content
- ✅ Maintains full backward compatibility

The solution is minimal, targeted, and follows the established patterns in the codebase while fixing the specific issue described in the GitHub issue.

{"file_path": "/project/test_fix.kts", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env kotlin

import java.net.URI

// Copy of the fixed getFileUri function
fun getFileUri(path: String): URI {
    var p = path
    if (java.io.File.separatorChar != '/') p = p.replace(java.io.File.separatorChar, '/')
    if (!p.startsWith("/")) p = "/$p"
    if (!p.startsWith("//")) p = "//$p"
    
    // Encode only specific characters that are problematic in file URIs  
    // % is used for percent-encoding so encode it first, then # (fragment delimiter)
    p = p.replace("%", "%25").replace("#", "%23")
    
    return URI("file", p, null)
}

// Test cases based on the existing tests
fun testCases() {
    println("Testing getFileUri fix...")
    
    // Test case 1: Path with special characters
    val path1 = "/storage/emulated/0/AnkiDroid@#$%/collection.media/magenta.png"
    val uri1 = getFileUri(path1)
    val expected1 = "file:///storage/emulated/0/AnkiDroid@%23$%25/collection.media/magenta.png"
    println("Test 1:")
    println("  Input: $path1")
    println("  Output: $uri1")
    println("  Expected: $expected1")
    println("  ✓ Pass: ${uri1.toString() == expected1}")
    println()
    
    // Test case 2: Filename with special characters
    val path2 = "/storage/emulated/0/AnkiDroid/collection.media/bregafunk@$%#.mp4"
    val uri2 = getFileUri(path2)
    val expected2 = "file:///storage/emulated/0/AnkiDroid/collection.media/bregafunk@$%25%23.mp4"
    println("Test 2:")
    println("  Input: $path2")
    println("  Output: $uri2")
    println("  Expected: $expected2")
    println("  ✓ Pass: ${uri2.toString() == expected2}")
    println()
    
    // Test case 3: Simple filename without special characters
    val path3 = "/storage/emulated/0/AnkiDroid/collection.media/simple.png"
    val uri3 = getFileUri(path3)
    val expected3 = "file:///storage/emulated/0/AnkiDroid/collection.media/simple.png"
    println("Test 3:")
    println("  Input: $path3")
    println("  Output: $uri3")
    println("  Expected: $expected3")
    println("  ✓ Pass: ${uri3.toString() == expected3}")
}

testCases()"}

396→    if (!p.startsWith("//")) p = "//$p"
397→    
398→    // Encode only specific characters that are problematic in file URIs  
399→    // % is used for percent-encoding so encode it first, then # (fragment delimiter)
400→    p = p.replace("%", "%25").replace("#", "%23")
401→    
402→    return URI("file", p, null)
